---
title: "Computing Concepts with Python"
subtitle: "Second Edition (2.0)"
author: "Brendan Shea, PhD"
date: last-modified
toc: false
---

A free, open-access introduction to computer science. Each week pairs a
hands-on Jupyter notebook with a short case study on the people,
arguments, and history behind the technical material. The notebooks
teach you to write Python with the help of an AI pair-programmer; the
case studies ask you to think about what that code does in the world.
Used in **COMP 1150** at Rochester Community and Technical College and
freely adaptable elsewhere under
[CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). The
materials here are the second edition (2.0); the original 2024
edition is preserved in the [archive](archive/index.qmd).

## Notebooks

Each notebook is available three ways: **Read** (accessible HTML in the
browser), **Run** (open in Google Colab to execute and edit), and
**Download** (the raw `.ipynb`).

| # | Notebook | Read | Run | Download |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | What Is Computing? | [HTML](v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB01_WhatIsComputing.html) | [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/blob/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB01_WhatIsComputing.ipynb) | [.ipynb](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB01_WhatIsComputing.ipynb) |
| 2 | Machine Architecture & Data Representation | [HTML](v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB02_MachineArchitecture.html) | [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/blob/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB02_MachineArchitecture.ipynb) | [.ipynb](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB02_MachineArchitecture.ipynb) |
| 3 | Python via Pseudocode & Flowcharts | [HTML](v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB03_PseudocodeFlowcharts.html) | [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/blob/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB03_PseudocodeFlowcharts.ipynb) | [.ipynb](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB03_PseudocodeFlowcharts.ipynb) |
| 4 | Control Flow & Functions | [HTML](v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB04_ControlFlowFunctions.html) | [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/blob/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB04_ControlFlowFunctions.ipynb) | [.ipynb](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB04_ControlFlowFunctions.ipynb) |
| 5 | Collections & ADTs | [HTML](v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB05_CollectionsADTs.html) | [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/blob/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB05_CollectionsADTs.ipynb) | [.ipynb](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB05_CollectionsADTs.ipynb) |
| 6 | Functions, Modules & OOP | [HTML](v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB06_ModulesOOP.html) | [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/blob/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB06_ModulesOOP.ipynb) | [.ipynb](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB06_ModulesOOP.ipynb) |
| 7 | Algorithms, Complexity & the Limits of Computation | [HTML](v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB07_SearchingSorting.html) | [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/blob/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB07_SearchingSorting.ipynb) | [.ipynb](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB07_SearchingSorting.ipynb) |
| 8 | Software Engineering, Git & AI-Assisted Dev | [HTML](v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB08_SoftwareEngineering.html) | [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/blob/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB08_SoftwareEngineering.ipynb) | [.ipynb](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB08_SoftwareEngineering.ipynb) |
| 9 | Databases: Relational & Non-Relational | [HTML](v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB09_Databases.html) | [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/blob/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB09_Databases.ipynb) | [.ipynb](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB09_Databases.ipynb) |
| 10 | OS, Networks, Cloud & the Web | [HTML](v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB10_OSNetworksWeb.html) | [Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/blob/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB10_OSNetworksWeb.ipynb) | [.ipynb](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/brendanpshea/computing_concepts_python/main/v2/notebooks/COMP1150_NB10_OSNetworksWeb.ipynb) |
| 11 | Cybersecurity & Secure Software Development | *in progress* | | |
| 12 | AI, Machine Learning & Ethics | *in progress* | | |

## Case studies

Short, discussion-ready pieces on the history, ethics, and unresolved
arguments behind the technical material. About twenty to thirty
minutes each. The ten drafted so far:

- [Lovelace and Turing: Can a Machine Originate?](v2/cases/cs01_lovelace_turing.qmd) —
  paired with Notebook 1.
- [The Silicon Shield: TSMC, Moore's Law, and the New Chip Wars](v2/cases/cs02_silicon_shield.qmd) —
  paired with Notebook 2.
- [Grace Hopper, Compilers & the Abstraction Bargain](v2/cases/cs03_hopper_compilers.qmd) —
  paired with Notebook 3.
- [The Therac-25: When Control Flow Kills](v2/cases/cs04_therac25.qmd) —
  paired with Notebook 4.
- [How We Represent People: ADTs and the Ethics of Data Modeling](v2/cases/cs05_representing_people.qmd) —
  paired with Notebook 5.
- [Was OOP a Mistake? Alan Kay, Smalltalk & the Backlash](v2/cases/cs06_oop_mistake.qmd) —
  paired with Notebook 6.
- ["We Must Know — We Will Know": Gödel, Turing, and the Problem No Computer Can Solve](v2/cases/cs07_halting_problem.qmd) —
  paired with Notebook 7.
- [Open Source and the Code an AI Learned From](v2/cases/cs08_open_source_copilot.qmd) —
  paired with Notebook 8.
- [From SQL to NoSQL: Why Google Broke the Database](v2/cases/cs09_sql_nosql.qmd) —
  paired with Notebook 9.
- [Three Companies Own the Internet](v2/cases/cs10_cloud_oligopoly.qmd) —
  paired with Notebook 10.

The full [case studies index](v2/cases/index.qmd) lists all twelve
planned cases with brief descriptions.

## How the pieces fit together

- **Read the notebook first.** Run the code. Do the exercises.
- **Then read the paired case study.** It tells the human story behind
  what you just learned and opens a debate worth your own opinion on.
- **Bring both to class.** The discussion questions are written to be
  argued out loud.

## For instructors

Everything here is CC BY 4.0 — adapt and remix freely. See
[About](about.qmd) for adoption notes, source layout, and contact info.
